---
title: "Advanced Feature Annotation using RuSirius"
output:
  rmarkdown::html_document
bibliography: advanced-feature-annotation-references.bib
author:
  - name: Metabonaut Developers
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Advanced Feature Annotation using RuSirius}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
  %\VignetteDepends{RuSirius,Spectra}
---

<!--
# Pre-render with (in the vignettes folder)
knitr::knit("advanced_feature_annotation_with_RuSirius.Rmd.orig", output = "advanced_feature_annotation_with_RuSirius.Rmd")
-->



**Note**: this vignette is
[**pre-computed**](https://ropensci.org/blog/2019/12/08/precompute-vignettes/).
See the session info for information on packages used and the date the vignette
was rendered. The vignette requires a running
[Sirius](https://bio.informatik.uni-jena.de/software/sirius/) instance and
a Sirius account for structure database searches. To reproduce this analysis,
you will need to log in to your own Sirius account (see the
[Prerequisites](#prerequisites) section below). This vignette was pre-rendered
using the authors' Sirius account; users must provide their own credentials.

# Introduction

In the main end-to-end LC-MS/MS untargeted metabolomics workflow, we
successfully preprocessed our data, performed statistical analysis, and
identified features with significant differences in abundance. However, matching
the MS1 and MS2 spectra directly against the MassBank database only confidently
annotated a single feature (caffeine).

As noted previously, this low proportion of annotated signals is common, and
external software tools such as SIRIUS can be excellent alternatives for
structure elucidation. SIRIUS uses sophisticated algorithms to predict molecular
formulas and structures *de novo* from isotope patterns and fragmentation trees.

This vignette will demonstrate how to seamlessly continue your `metabonaut`
analysis by passing your unannotated significant features into Sirius using the
`RuSirius` R package.

# Prerequisites

To run this workflow interactively, you must have:

1. **Sirius 6.3** installed and running on your system.
2. The `RuSirius` package installed and loaded.
3. A **Sirius account** (free registration at
   [bright-giant.com](https://bright-giant.com/)) — required for structure
   database searches.


``` r
library(RuSirius)
library(Spectra)
```

# Loading Previous Data

In the end-to-end workflow, we isolated the MS2 spectra for all significant
features, concatenated them into a single `Spectra` object, and saved them to
disk. We will load this exact object to begin our advanced annotation.


``` r
#' Load the Spectra object containing MS2 scans of significant features
load(system.file("extdata", "spectra_significant_fts.RData",
                 package = "Metabonaut"))

#' Verify the loaded object
ms2_ctr_fts
#> MSn data (Spectra) with 315 spectra in a MsBackendMemory backend:
#>       msLevel     rtime scanIndex
#>     <integer> <numeric> <integer>
#> 1           2   147.357      2043
#> 2           2   148.587      2061
#> ...       ...       ...       ...
#> 314         2   181.822      2535
#> 315         2   183.072      2553
#>  ... 39 more variables/columns.
#> Processing:
#>  Filter: select retention time [10..240] on MS level(s) 1 2 [Tue Mar 18 11:56:42 2025]
#>  Filter: select MS level(s) 2 [Tue Mar 18 11:56:50 2025]
#>  Remove peaks based on their intensities and a user-provided function in spectra of MS level(s) 2. [Tue Mar 18 11:56:50 2025]
#>  ...19 more processings. Use 'processingLog' to list all.
```

Each spectrum in this object already contains a `feature_id` variable that links
it back to our main `XcmsExperiment` results.

# Connecting to Sirius and Importing Data

We start by establishing a connection to the Sirius application using the
`Sirius()` function. This function will either connect to a running *Sirius*
instance, or start a new one and connect to that. Parameter `port` used below
allows to configure to *Sirius* application to use a particular port, but
generally the function can be used without specifying a port.

We will create a new project dedicated to annotating these specific features.


``` r
#' Initialize the Sirius connection and create a new project
srs <- Sirius(projectId = "metabonaut_significant_features",
              path = getwd(), port = 9999)
#> Found SIRIUS in PATH! Using this information to start the application.
#> SIRIUS was started without specifying --port (-p), trying to find the sirius.port file.

#' Check connection status
checkConnection(srs)
#> [1] TRUE
```

If you have not already logged in during the `Sirius()` call (by passing
`username` and `password` arguments), you must log in before running any
structure database searches:


``` r
#' Log in to your Sirius account
srs <- logIn(srs,
             username = "your_email@example.com",
             password = "your_password")
```

Next, we import our `Spectra` object into the Sirius project. We map the
`ms_column_name` parameter to `"feature_id"` so that Sirius uses our existing
feature names.


``` r
#' Import the MS2 spectra into Sirius
srs <- import(
    sirius = srs,
    spectra = ms2_ctr_fts,
    ms_column_name = "feature_id",
    deleteExistingFeatures = TRUE
)

#' View summary of imported features
head(featuresInfo(srs))
#>      alignedFeatureId     compoundId           externalFeatureId ionMass
#> [1,] "817374840184846592" "817374840172263675" "FT0371"          138.0548
#> [2,] "817374840205818113" "817374840172263676" "FT0565"          161.0401
#> [3,] "817374840218401026" "817374840172263677" "FT0732"          182.0748
#> [4,] "817374840239372547" "817374840172263678" "FT0845"          195.0877
#> [5,] "817374840251955460" "817374840172263679" "FT1171"          229.1299
#>      charge detectedAdducts hasMs1 hasMsMs computing
#> [1,] 1      list,1          FALSE  TRUE    FALSE
#> [2,] 1      list,1          FALSE  TRUE    FALSE
#> [3,] 1      list,1          FALSE  TRUE    FALSE
#> [4,] 1      list,1          FALSE  TRUE    FALSE
#> [5,] 1      list,1          FALSE  TRUE    FALSE
```

# Running Sirius Computations

With the data imported, we can submit a job to Sirius. We will ask Sirius to:

1. Identify candidate molecular formulas (`formulaIdParams`).
2. Predict the compound class (`predictParams`).
3. Search structure databases for candidate structures
   (`structureDbSearchParams`).

Because our samples are human serum/plasma analyzed in positive polarity, we
expect protonated ions (`[M+H]+`), sodium adducts (`[M+Na]+`), and ammonium
adducts (`[M+H-NH3]+`). We supply these as our fallback adducts.


``` r
#' Submit the annotation job
job_id <- run(
    srs,
    fallbackAdducts = c("[M+H]+", "[M+Na]+", "[M+H-NH3]+"),
    formulaIdParams = formulaIdParam(
        numberOfCandidates = 5,
        instrument = "QTOF",
        massAccuracyMS2ppm = 10
    ),
    predictParams = predictParam(),
    structureDbSearchParams = structureDbSearchParam(
        structureSearchDbs = c("BIO")
    ),
    recompute = TRUE,
    wait = TRUE
)

#' Optional: Print job info if you want to verify successful completion
jobInfo(srs, job_id)
#> [1] "Job ID: 1\n\nCommand: \n--IsotopeSettings.filter=true\n--InjectSpectralLibraryMatchFormulas.minPeakMatchesToInject=6\n--FormulaSettings.enforced=HCNOP\n--InjectSpectralLibraryMatchFormulas.injectFormulas=true\n--TagStructuresByElGordo=true\n--AdductSettings.detectable=[M+H3N+H]+,[M-H4O2+H]+,[M-H2O-H]-,[M-H3N-H]-,[M+Cl]-,[2M+K]+,[M+K]+,[2M+Cl]-,[M+C2H4O2-H]-,[M+H]+,[2M+H]+,[M-CH3-H]-,[M-H]-,[M+Na]+,[M-H2O+H]+\n--RecomputeResults=true\n--UseHeuristic.useHeuristicAboveMz=300\n--IsotopeMs2Settings=IGNORE\n--MS2MassDeviation.allowedMassDeviation=10.0ppm\n--FormulaSearchSettings.applyFormulaConstraintsToDatabaseCandidates=false\n--EnforceElGordoFormula=true\n--NumberOfCandidatesPerIonization=1\n--AdductSettings.fallback=[M+H]+,[M+Na]+,[M+H-NH3]+\n--FormulaSearchSettings.performBottomUpAboveMz=0.0\n--FormulaSettings.fallback=S\n--FormulaSearchSettings.applyFormulaConstraintsToBottomUp=false\n--UseHeuristic.useOnlyHeuristicAboveMz=650\n--ExpansiveSearchConfidenceMode.confidenceScoreSimilarityMode=APPROXIMATE\n--InjectSpectralLibraryMatchFormulas.minScoreToInject=0.7\n--FormulaSearchDB=\n--FormulaResultThreshold=true\n--InjectSpectralLibraryMatchFormulas.alwaysPredict=false\n--FormulaSettings.detectable=B,S,Cl,Se,Br\n--NumberOfCandidates=5\nformulas\nfingerprints\nclasses\nstructures\n\nProgress:\n   State: DONE\n   Current Progress: 2650\n   Max Progress: 2650\n\nAffected Compound IDs:\n   817374840172263679, 817374840172263678, 817374840172263677, 817374840172263676, 817374840172263675\n\nAffected Aligned Feature IDs:\n817374840251955460\n817374840239372547\n817374840218401026\n817374840205818113\n817374840184846592\n"
```

*Tip: If you want to explore the data visually while or after it processes, you
can use `openGUI(srs)` to launch the Sirius graphical interface!*

# Retrieving and Interpreting Results

Once the computation is complete, we can extract the structural and formula
predictions back into R for downstream analysis.

### High-Level Summary

The `summary()` function provides a quick overview of the top formulas,
structures, and compound classes predicted for each feature. Pay close attention
to the confidence scores.


``` r
summary_results <- summary(sirius = srs, result.type = "structure")
head(summary_results)
#>     alignedFeatureId         compoundId externalFeatureId  ionMass charge
#> 1 817374840184846592 817374840172263675            FT0371 138.0548      1
#> 2 817374840205818113 817374840172263676            FT0565 161.0401      1
#> 3 817374840218401026 817374840172263677            FT0732 182.0748      1
#> 4 817374840239372547 817374840172263678            FT0845 195.0877      1
#> 5 817374840251955460 817374840172263679            FT1171 229.1299      1
#>   hasMs1 hasMsMs          formulaId molecularFormula    adduct rank
#> 1  FALSE    TRUE 817374847399049490          C7H7NO2  [M + H]+    3
#> 2  FALSE    TRUE 817374847843645729        C4H5BFNO4  [M + H]+    1
#> 3  FALSE    TRUE 817374849072576810       C5H9F2N3O2  [M + H]+    1
#> 4  FALSE    TRUE 817374847713622296        C8H10N4O2  [M + H]+    1
#> 5  FALSE    TRUE 817374847281608971        C12H18N2O [M + Na]+    2
#>   siriusScoreNormalized siriusScore isotopeScore treeScore       inchiKey
#> 1            0.04957077    20.13407            0  20.13407 VOCKNCWQVHJMAE
#> 2            0.50000000    69.24605            0  69.24605           <NA>
#> 3            0.49968135   112.21981            0 112.21981 GWRLHZIGYXCDKL
#> 4            0.48622197    68.84529            0  68.84529 RYYVLZVUVIJVGH
#> 5            0.08134845     5.47353            0   5.47353 HBIDZSUDZACENV
#>                             smiles
#> 1               COC1=NC=CC(=C1)C=O
#> 2                             <NA>
#> 3             C(CF)NC(=O)N(CCF)N=O
#> 4     CN1C=NC2=C1C(=O)N(C(=O)N2C)C
#> 5 CC(=CCCC(=CCC(=O)C=[N+]=[N-])C)C
#>                                   structureName      xlogP rank.1   csiScore
#> 1                   2-Methoxyisonicotinaldehyde  0.9654924      1  -75.15435
#> 2                                          <NA>         NA     NA         NA
#> 3                   Bis(fluoroethyl)nitrosourea  0.8681960      1 -152.63122
#> 4                                         Thein -0.1085821      1  -12.05606
#> 5 (4E)-1-Diazo-5,9-dimethyl-4,8-decadiene-2-one  3.4000000      1 -125.16005
#>   tanimotoSimilarity mcesDistToTopHit type   level levelIndex
#> 1          0.2592593                0  NPC PATHWAY          0
#> 2                 NA               NA  NPC PATHWAY          0
#> 3          0.3125000                0  NPC PATHWAY          0
#> 4          0.9743590                0  NPC PATHWAY          0
#> 5          0.3018868                0  NPC PATHWAY          0
#>                       name                       description id probability
#> 1                Alkaloids                Pathway: Alkaloids  0   0.5230578
#> 2 Amino acids and Peptides Pathway: Amino acids and Peptides  1   0.8786789
#> 3                Alkaloids                Pathway: Alkaloids  0   0.1652622
#> 4                Alkaloids                Pathway: Alkaloids  0   0.9989780
#> 5               Terpenoids               Pathway: Terpenoids  6   0.5267499
#>   index type.1    level.1 levelIndex.1                           name.1
#> 1     0    NPC SUPERCLASS            1         Nicotinic acid alkaloids
#> 2     1    NPC SUPERCLASS            1                   Small peptides
#> 3     0    NPC SUPERCLASS            1                   Small peptides
#> 4     0    NPC SUPERCLASS            1 Pseudoalkaloids (transamidation)
#> 5     6    NPC SUPERCLASS            1                 Sesquiterpenoids
#>                                  description.1 id.1 probability.1 index.1
#> 1         Superclass: Nicotinic acid alkaloids   44     0.6076760      44
#> 2                   Superclass: Small peptides   63     0.8957072      63
#> 3                   Superclass: Small peptides   63     0.1016899      63
#> 4 Superclass: Pseudoalkaloids (transamidation)   59     0.9999007      59
#> 5                 Superclass: Sesquiterpenoids   61     0.1907384      61
#>   type.2 level.2 levelIndex.2              name.2              description.2
#> 1    NPC   CLASS            2  Pyridine alkaloids  Class: Pyridine alkaloids
#> 2    NPC   CLASS            2          Aminoacids          Class: Aminoacids
#> 3    NPC   CLASS            2 Imidazole alkaloids Class: Imidazole alkaloids
#> 4    NPC   CLASS            2    Purine alkaloids    Class: Purine alkaloids
#> 5    NPC   CLASS            2 Terpenoid alkaloids Class: Terpenoid alkaloids
#>   id.2 probability.2 index.2 confidenceExactMatch confidenceApproxMatch
#> 1  602    0.60770983     602           0.06000814            0.06000814
#> 2  109    0.93744338     109                   NA                    NA
#> 3  399    0.08924162     399           0.02842510            0.02842510
#> 4  597    0.99996340     597           0.67976562            0.89135261
#> 5  682    0.15184069     682           0.03929589            0.03929589
#>   expansiveSearchState computing
#> 1                  OFF     FALSE
#> 2                 <NA>     FALSE
#> 3                  OFF     FALSE
#> 4                  OFF     FALSE
#> 5          APPROXIMATE     FALSE
```

### Detailed Structure Database Search Results

To dive deeper into the top structural candidates for a specific formula, we use
the `results()` function. We will extract the top 2 structures for the top 3
formulas per feature as a data frame.


``` r
structure_hits <- results(
    srs,
    return.type = "data.frame",
    result.type = "structureDb",
    topFormula = 3,
    topStructure = 2
)

head(structure_hits)
#>            formulaId molecularFormula         adduct rank.x
#> 1 817374847399049488       C4H10ClN2O       [M + H]+      1
#> 2 817374847399049489       C4H13ClN3O [M - H3N + H]+      2
#> 3 817374847399049490          C7H7NO2       [M + H]+      3
#> 4 817374847399049490          C7H7NO2       [M + H]+      3
#> 5 817374847843645729        C4H5BFNO4       [M + H]+     NA
#> 6 817374847843645730       C4H8BFN2O4 [M - H3N + H]+     NA
#>   siriusScoreNormalized siriusScore isotopeScore treeScore xcms_fts
#> 1            0.42161537    22.27476            0  22.27476   FT0371
#> 2            0.42161537    22.27476            0  22.27476   FT0371
#> 3            0.04957077    20.13407            0  20.13407   FT0371
#> 4            0.04957077    20.13407            0  20.13407   FT0371
#> 5            0.50000000    69.24605            0  69.24605   FT0565
#> 6            0.50000000    69.24605            0  69.24605   FT0565
#>         inchiKey               smiles                     structureName
#> 1           <NA>                 <NA>                              <NA>
#> 2           <NA>                 <NA>                              <NA>
#> 3 VOCKNCWQVHJMAE   COC1=NC=CC(=C1)C=O       2-Methoxyisonicotinaldehyde
#> 4 REYSBWKWFPSDDR CC(=O)C1=C(C=NC=C1)O 1-(3-Hydroxypyridin-4-yl)ethanone
#> 5           <NA>                 <NA>                              <NA>
#> 6           <NA>                 <NA>                              <NA>
#>       xlogP rank.y  csiScore tanimotoSimilarity mcesDistToTopHit
#> 1        NA     NA        NA                 NA               NA
#> 2        NA     NA        NA                 NA               NA
#> 3 0.9654924      1 -75.15435          0.2592593                0
#> 4 0.7000000      2 -84.18843          0.3157895              Inf
#> 5        NA     NA        NA                 NA               NA
#> 6        NA     NA        NA                 NA               NA
#>           sirius_fts rank
#> 1 817374840184846592   NA
#> 2 817374840184846592   NA
#> 3 817374840184846592   NA
#> 4 817374840184846592   NA
#> 5 817374840205818113    1
#> 6 817374840205818113    2
```

You can seamlessly merge these results back into your `rowData(res)` from the
main workflow using the `feature_id` to complement your differential abundance
statistics with high-confidence structural predictions.

### Compound Class Predictions

Sometimes Sirius cannot confidently predict an exact structure, but it can
accurately determine the broader compound class (e.g., "Amino acids and
derivatives" or "Lipids").


``` r
class_predictions <- results(
    srs,
    return.type = "data.frame",
    result.type = "compoundClass",
    topFormula = 1
)

head(class_predictions)
#>            formulaId molecularFormula   adduct rank siriusScoreNormalized
#> 1 817374847399049488       C4H10ClN2O [M + H]+    1             0.4216154
#> 2 817374847399049488       C4H10ClN2O [M + H]+    1             0.4216154
#> 3 817374847399049488       C4H10ClN2O [M + H]+    1             0.4216154
#> 4 817374847399049488       C4H10ClN2O [M + H]+    1             0.4216154
#> 5 817374847399049488       C4H10ClN2O [M + H]+    1             0.4216154
#> 6 817374847399049488       C4H10ClN2O [M + H]+    1             0.4216154
#>   siriusScore isotopeScore treeScore xcms_fts           section       type
#> 1    22.27476            0  22.27476   FT0371        npcPathway        NPC
#> 2    22.27476            0  22.27476   FT0371     npcSuperclass        NPC
#> 3    22.27476            0  22.27476   FT0371          npcClass        NPC
#> 4    22.27476            0  22.27476   FT0371 classyFireLineage ClassyFire
#> 5    22.27476            0  22.27476   FT0371 classyFireLineage ClassyFire
#> 6    22.27476            0  22.27476   FT0371 classyFireLineage ClassyFire
#>        level levelIndex                       name
#> 1    PATHWAY          0                  Alkaloids
#> 2 SUPERCLASS          1        Ornithine alkaloids
#> 3      CLASS          2    Halogenated fatty acids
#> 4    Kingdom          1          Organic compounds
#> 5 Superclass          2 Organic nitrogen compounds
#> 6      Class          3   Organonitrogen compounds
#>                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            description
#> 1                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   Pathway: Alkaloids
#> 2                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      Superclass: Ornithine alkaloids
#> 3                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       Class: Halogenated fatty acids
#> 4 Compounds that contain at least carbon atom, excluding isocyanide/cyanide and their non-hydrocarbyl derivatives, thiophosgene, carbon diselenide, carbon monosulfide, carbon disulfide, carbon subsulfide, carbon monoxide, carbon trioxide, Carbon suboxide, and dicarbon monoxide.
#> 5                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        Organic compounds containing a nitrogen atom.
#> 6                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        Organic compounds containing a nitrogen atom.
#>     id probability index parentId                 parentName         sirius_fts
#> 1    0  0.54134756     0       NA                       <NA> 817374840184846592
#> 2   48  0.10274610    48       NA                       <NA> 817374840184846592
#> 3  376  0.07766195   376       NA                       <NA> 817374840184846592
#> 4    0  0.99994910     0  9999999          Chemical entities 817374840184846592
#> 5 4707  0.99845815  4699        0          Organic compounds 817374840184846592
#> 6  278  0.99627054   278     4707 Organic nitrogen compounds 817374840184846592
```

# Clean Up

Once you have saved your results to your R environment, it is good practice to
cleanly shut down the Sirius connection.


``` r
#' Close the project and shut down the Sirius connection
shutdown(srs)
#> Sirius was shut down successfully
```

# Summary

By integrating `RuSirius` into the `metabonaut` workflow, we easily transitioned
from unresolved MS2 spectra to powerful structural predictions. This bridges the
gap between raw statistical feature discovery and biological interpretation,
allowing you to annotate features that simple spectral library matching might
miss.


# References and Acknowledgements

This vignette relies on the
[SIRIUS](https://bio.informatik.uni-jena.de/software/sirius/) software suite
developed by the [Böcker Lab](https://bio.informatik.uni-jena.de/) at
Friedrich-Schiller-Universität Jena and [Bright Giant
GmbH](https://bright-giant.com/). SIRIUS integrates several algorithms for
metabolite annotation from high-resolution mass spectrometry data. When using
SIRIUS and the tools accessed through this workflow, please cite the following
references:

- **SIRIUS** — fragmentation tree computation and molecular formula
  identification [@duhrkop_sirius_2019].

- **CSI:FingerID** — molecular structure database search
  [@duhrkop_csifingerid_2015].

- **COSMIC** — confidence scoring for structural annotations
  [@hoffmann_cosmic_2022].

- **CANOPUS** — compound class prediction from fragmentation spectra
  [@duhrkop_canopus_2021].

- **ZODIAC** — molecular formula re-ranking using Gibbs sampling
  [@ludwig_zodiac_2020].

- **MSNovelist** — *de novo* structure generation from mass spectra
  [@stravs_msnovelist_2022].

The R interface to SIRIUS is provided by the
[RuSirius](https://github.com/RforMassSpectrometry/RuSirius) package (Louail
*et al.*), which is built upon the
[RSirius](https://github.com/sirius-ms/sirius-client-openAPI/tree/master/client-api_r)
REST API library. Special thanks to Markus Fleischauer for his work on the
Sirius SDKs, Jonas Emmert for making the R API usable, and Marcus Ludwig for
support in implementing RuSirius.

# Session information

The R code was run on:


``` r
date()
#> [1] "Thu Mar  5 13:36:23 2026"
```

Information on the R session:


``` r
sessionInfo()
#> R version 4.5.2 (2025-10-31)
#> Platform: x86_64-pc-linux-gnu
#> Running under: Arch Linux
#>
#> Matrix products: default
#> BLAS:   /home/jo/R/R-4.5.2/lib64/R/lib/libRblas.so
#> LAPACK: /usr/lib/liblapack.so.3.12.0  LAPACK version 3.12.0
#>
#> locale:
#>  [1] LC_CTYPE=en_US.UTF-8       LC_NUMERIC=C
#>  [3] LC_TIME=en_US.UTF-8        LC_COLLATE=en_US.UTF-8
#>  [5] LC_MONETARY=en_US.UTF-8    LC_MESSAGES=en_US.UTF-8
#>  [7] LC_PAPER=en_US.UTF-8       LC_NAME=C
#>  [9] LC_ADDRESS=C               LC_TELEPHONE=C
#> [11] LC_MEASUREMENT=en_US.UTF-8 LC_IDENTIFICATION=C
#>
#> time zone: Europe/Rome
#> tzcode source: system (glibc)
#>
#> attached base packages:
#> [1] stats4    stats     graphics  grDevices utils     datasets  methods
#> [8] base
#>
#> other attached packages:
#> [1] RuSirius_0.2.4      jsonlite_2.0.0      Spectra_1.20.1
#> [4] BiocParallel_1.44.0 S4Vectors_0.48.0    BiocGenerics_0.56.0
#> [7] generics_0.1.4      RSirius_6.3.3       ProtGenerics_1.42.0
#>
#> loaded via a namespace (and not attached):
#>   [1] DBI_1.3.0                   bitops_1.0-9
#>   [3] MetaboAnnotation_1.14.0     gridExtra_2.3
#>   [5] httr2_1.2.2                 remotes_2.5.0
#>   [7] rlang_1.1.7                 magrittr_2.0.4
#>   [9] clue_0.3-67                 otel_0.2.0
#>  [11] matrixStats_1.5.0           compiler_4.5.2
#>  [13] RSQLite_2.4.6               png_0.1-8
#>  [15] callr_3.7.6                 vctrs_0.7.1
#>  [17] reshape2_1.4.5              stringr_1.6.0
#>  [19] crayon_1.5.3                pkgconfig_2.0.3
#>  [21] MetaboCoreUtils_1.18.1      fastmap_1.2.0
#>  [23] dbplyr_2.5.2                XVector_0.50.0
#>  [25] ps_1.9.1                    purrr_1.2.1
#>  [27] bit_4.6.0                   xfun_0.56
#>  [29] MultiAssayExperiment_1.36.1 cachem_1.1.0
#>  [31] ChemmineR_3.62.0            blob_1.3.0
#>  [33] DelayedArray_0.36.0         parallel_4.5.2
#>  [35] cluster_2.1.8.2             R6_2.6.1
#>  [37] stringi_1.8.7               RColorBrewer_1.1-3
#>  [39] GenomicRanges_1.62.1        Rcpp_1.1.1
#>  [41] Seqinfo_1.0.0               SummarizedExperiment_1.40.0
#>  [43] knitr_1.51                  base64enc_0.1-6
#>  [45] IRanges_2.44.0              BiocBaseUtils_1.12.0
#>  [47] Matrix_1.7-4                igraph_2.2.2
#>  [49] tidyselect_1.2.1            dichromat_2.0-0.1
#>  [51] abind_1.4-8                 yaml_2.3.12
#>  [53] codetools_0.2-20            curl_7.0.0
#>  [55] processx_3.8.6              pkgbuild_1.4.8
#>  [57] lattice_0.22-9              tibble_3.3.1
#>  [59] plyr_1.8.9                  KEGGREST_1.50.0
#>  [61] Biobase_2.70.0              S7_0.2.1
#>  [63] evaluate_1.0.5              desc_1.4.3
#>  [65] BiocFileCache_3.0.0         xml2_1.5.2
#>  [67] Biostrings_2.78.0           pillar_1.11.1
#>  [69] BiocManager_1.30.27         filelock_1.0.3
#>  [71] MatrixGenerics_1.22.0       DT_0.34.0
#>  [73] RCurl_1.98-1.17             BiocVersion_3.22.0
#>  [75] ggplot2_4.0.2               scales_1.4.0
#>  [77] glue_1.8.0                  lazyeval_0.2.2
#>  [79] tools_4.5.2                 AnnotationHub_4.0.0
#>  [81] data.table_1.18.2.1         QFeatures_1.20.0
#>  [83] fs_1.6.6                    grid_4.5.2
#>  [85] tidyr_1.3.2                 MsCoreUtils_1.22.1
#>  [87] AnnotationDbi_1.72.0        cli_3.6.5
#>  [89] rappdirs_0.3.4              rsvg_2.7.0
#>  [91] S4Arrays_1.10.1             dplyr_1.2.0
#>  [93] AnnotationFilter_1.34.0     gtable_0.3.6
#>  [95] digest_0.6.39               SparseArray_1.10.8
#>  [97] rjson_0.2.23                htmlwidgets_1.6.4
#>  [99] farver_2.1.2                memoise_2.0.1
#> [101] htmltools_0.5.9             lifecycle_1.0.5
#> [103] httr_1.4.8                  CompoundDb_1.15.3
#> [105] bit64_4.6.0-1               MASS_7.3-65
```
